_template_instructions: Replace every <PLACEHOLDER> value with board-specific data.
name: <board_id>_zephyr_<sample_name>

preflight._note: MUST be false — Zephyr uses west/OpenOCD directly, not the AEL instrument stack

notes: <Describe what this sample proves, which peripheral/pin is used, Zephyr version>

build._note_type: Tells AEL to use ZephyrBackend via build_zephyr adapter
build._note_zephyr_board: Run: west boards | grep <chip_family> to find this
build.project_dir: <absolute path OR repo-relative path to Zephyr app dir>
build._note_project_dir: For upstream samples use absolute path: /home/aes/zephyrproject/zephyr/samples/<name>. For AEL firmware use repo-relative: firmware/targets/<target>/
build._note_build_dir: Repo-relative; will be created under repo root

flash._note_method: Routes to load.zephyr_west in adapter_registry → flash_zephyr.run()
flash._note_runner: From board.cmake: openocd (most STM32) | jlink | pyocd. Check boards/<arch>/<board>/board.cmake
flash._optional_openocd_config: Uncomment if board's openocd.cfg hardcodes ST-Link but you have CMSIS-DAP:

observe_uart._note_baud: 115200 is the Zephyr default for all STM32 boards
observe_uart._note_profile: stm32 = no boot ROM noise filtering. Use esp32 for ESP32 targets.
observe_uart._note_duration_s: Must be long enough to capture at least 2 pattern occurrences. 8s = ~8 prints at 500ms cadence

serial_console.notes: USB-UART adapter: DUT <CONSOLE_TX_PIN> -> RXD, GND -> GND

peripheral_signals.dut_signal: <PA2/USART2_TX or PA9/USART1_TX — check DTS chosen node>
peripheral_signals.notes: Zephyr console UART TX. Connect to USB-UART adapter RXD.
